content.summary: Uses the audio frequency spectrum to displace geometry vertices in real time. An Audio Spectrum CHOP extracts FFT frequency bands, which are converted to a texture via CHOP to TOP or used directly as CHOP data to drive a Noise SOP or Point SOP displacement. This creates geometry that physically responds to different frequencies in the music.

operatorChain.role: match sample count to geometry resolution

codeSnippets.label: Configure audio spectrum for displacement mapping
codeSnippets.code:
# Set up audio spectrum analysis for geometry displacement
spectrum = op('audiospectrum1')
resample = op('resample1')
chop_to_top = op('chopto1')

# Configure spectrum
spectrum.par.outputop = 'magnitude'

# Resample to match grid resolution (e.g., 100 columns)
resample.par.method = 'timeslice'
resample.par.numsamples = 100
resample.par.interpolation = 'cubic'

# CHOP to TOP outputs a 1-pixel-tall texture with spectrum data
chop_to_top.par.format = 'rgba32float'
codeSnippets.description: Configures the spectrum analysis chain and converts it to a texture suitable for vertex displacement.

tips: Use a Resample CHOP to match the number of spectrum samples to your geometry resolution for clean mapping.
Apply a Math CHOP with a power function to compress the dynamic range of the spectrum before displacement.
Use logarithmic frequency scaling on the Audio Spectrum CHOP for more musically meaningful displacement.
Add a Trail CHOP before the CHOP to TOP to create a 2D spectrogram texture that scrolls over time.
